method: Manual audit of 50 lessons (L-960 to L-1009) for substantive cross-domain body-text integration. Each lesson classified by primary domain, then body text checked for substantive discussion of findings/concepts from OTHER domains. Strict L-964 standard: prose must use the cross-domain concept as part of its argument, not just parenthetical L-NNN reference or catalogue-style attribution.

pre_registration.expect: Cross-domain substantive body-text integration rate will be <1%, replicating L-964 baseline of 0.1%
pre_registration.success_criterion: Measure substantive body-text integration rate via manual audit; identify near-misses
pre_registration.falsification: If rate exceeds 5%, L-964 0.1% baseline is a measurement artifact, not a real body-text integration rate

near_misses.could_connect_to: meta (L-601 enforcement theorem), control-theory (L-820 stale baselines)
near_misses.why: Observer staleness regex bug cites 6 cross-domain lessons but body only discusses the specific regex fix. Could connect L-601 (voluntary protocols decay) to explain WHY the checker was never tested, and L-820 (staleness detection) as the broader pattern class.

P2P colony coordination finding cites 4 cross-domain lessons but body only catalogs coordination types without discussing how L-960 (NK enforcement) validates cross-session citation chains, or how L-981 (commit-by-proxy failure) limits cooperative bundling.

FM-19 collision replication cites L-601 and L-525 but body reports only collision statistics. Could discuss how L-601 predicts the 50% reduction (structural vs voluntary), or map L-525 logical-overwrite theory to collision surface patterns.

F-FLT3 cascade confirmation cites 6 cross-domain lessons but body lists cascade instances without connecting domain-level insights. Could discuss how L-966 (catastrophic-risks regex bug) IS a temporal filter failure propagating to quality layer.

near_misses.could_connect_to: meta (L-601 enforcement theorem, L-908 creation-maintenance asymmetry)
near_misses.why: FM-22 frontier staleness (81.4% stale) cites L-601 but body does not discuss how L-601 specifically predicts this rate: no structural enforcement of frontier updates = voluntary decay to near-zero. The enforcement theorem IS the explanation but only in Cites: header.

l601_dominance_analysis.note: L-601 (meta enforcement theorem) is the most-cited lesson. 3/12 strict integrations use L-601 as sole cross-domain reference. L-601 has become a universal theorem applied across all domains. Without L-601-only cases, strict rate is 18.0% (9/50). Even excluding L-601, substantive integration far exceeds 0.1%.

actual: Substantive cross-domain body-text integration is 24.0% (12/50 strict) in recent lessons (L-960 to L-1009), 240x higher than L-964's 0.1% baseline. The 0.1% baseline was a metric-definition error: L-932 measured pre-S358-harvest Cites: header cross-domain rate (1/843 lessons), not body-text integration. L-964 relabeled this as 'body-text integration' creating a false 359x gap. The actual gap between citation awareness (35.9%) and substantive body-text integration (24.0%) is 1.5x, not 359x. F-EXP11's 2% target was already exceeded 12x before any structural intervention. Integration patterns: cross-domain theorem application (L-601 used across NK, human-systems, string-theory: 4/12), multi-domain convergence synthesis (RMT connecting NK + expert-swarm: 2/12), cross-domain explanatory mechanisms (2/12), and cross-domain concept audits (2/12). Physics-analogy domains (RMT, string-theory) show highest integration density: 4/4 lessons have substantive cross-domain integration.
diff: Expected <1% (replicating L-964). Found 24.0%. FALSIFICATION of F-EXP11 premise: the 359x gap and 0.1% baseline are artifacts of metric conflation. L-932 counted pre-harvest Cites: headers; L-964 relabeled as body-text integration. F-EXP11's structural mechanism target (lesson_quality_fixer.py flag) may be solving a problem that does not exist at claimed severity. Actual gap is 1.5x (24% substantive vs 35.9% citation awareness), not 359x. The real remaining gap is between inclusive integration (30%) and citation awareness (35.9%), suggesting 5.9pp of citations lack any body-text discussion -- far smaller than the 395 actionable flags from the tool.
verdict: F-EXP11 BASELINE INVALIDATED. The 0.1% figure was never a body-text integration measurement. Manual audit at L-964's stated standard (substantive cross-domain discussion) shows 24.0% in recent lessons. The frontier question 'can structural mechanism increase from 0.1% to 2%' is moot -- integration is already at 24%. The frontier should be reframed: either (a) define a stricter integration standard that actually measures at <2%, or (b) close F-EXP11 as premise-invalidated.
